rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+) — student leaves chair while agent works
One exercise = one bounded activity
Student agency — adults decide; opportunity ≠ compulsion
Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens
Artifact-realism check — named tools/files must exist at this point
Teach the principle and the technique — default
Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go — and forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose
Corporate infra is nice-to-have, never a forcing function
Personal-first, team promotion via human conversation
Cohort-delivered exercises run on trainer-paced concurrent execution, not sequential
Host-skill is optional; default cohort is no host-skill
Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write
Classroom is the default delivery mode; the body teaches the work, the trainer manages the room
Reading burden for verification is banned — Claude audits Claude
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:115 — 'Open `response.html` and look at it. One thing should look more like the source. One thing should still be worth improving.' / line:139 — 'Look at the file again.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace manual look-and-assess with a short Claude-audits-Claude prompt that outputs the two findings; foreshadows the verification principle instead of asking the student to eyeball correctness.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Offered structures need a forced-engagement mechanic — defaults and plans alike get rubber-stamped
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:62-83 — Phase 2 prompt asks Claude to 'propose `styling-rules.md` in chat first' with no forced-engagement mechanic before the student accepts; student is implicitly expected to approve the proposed rules without a named customisation move.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a one-line forced-customisation callout (e.g. 'Pick at least one rule to rewrite before saving') adjacent to the Phase 2 prompt so the articulation goal lands for the ~90% who would otherwise accept verbatim.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: When task selection drives the learning outcome, name both floor AND ceiling explicitly
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:8-9 — 'Good default: a simple HTML explainer you could send internally. Other options: ...' — positive examples only; no bolded anti-pattern callout naming what NOT to bring.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a one-line anti-pattern callout naming the ceiling violation (e.g. 'Not a full site redesign or a complex app — one page, one answer.') so students with ambitious instincts know the scope boundary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audience tier determines authoring substrate
Plug points + UX — file must be both plug-able and operable
Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes
rules_evaluated.evidence: The maintainer block contains no Leap Test section with three observable Monday-morning outcomes. The block only has Meta, Quality, and Strategy reference lines.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a Leap Test entry in the maintainer block with three named observable verbs (e.g. 'opens a new Cowork session on the same folder and sees CLAUDE.md pre-load the style rules', 'adds one new rule to styling-rules.md without recreating the system', 'knows where to steer the style gap without reading the chat again').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function names its dominant failure and one move that recovers
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block has no failure-mode + escape hatch rows for the forcing functions in Phase 1 (gap-name audit), Phase 2 (style-system creation), and Phase 4 (rule-addition + regenerate).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a failure-modes section in the maintainer block with one dominant failure + escape hatch per phase that carries a forcing function; at minimum Phase 1 (student skips audit, takes generic output), Phase 2 (student pastes a vague style source that produces trivial rules), Phase 4 (student adds a duplicate of an existing rule).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Heavy fan-out prompts get a body-side recovery callout
Convenience-driven prompts with known shape-bias get body-side acknowledgment callouts
Session widget — `**Session** *(verb, "name")*` source shape; place by learning flow
Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:44 — 'The gap between generic and yours is the point of Phase 1; let Claude name it.' — body prose around the Phase 1 audit prompt previews the felt-evidence (naming the gap) that the prompt is supposed to surface.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cut 'let Claude name it' — the prompt produces the naming; body prose should only name the artifact ('an audit of five styling choices') not the revelation. The felt-evidence lands in session, not in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Multi-session ships as packaging, not as a new taught primitive
`/clear` is a tool with a purpose, not a ceremony
